thesis: FRMO Corporation is a Delaware holding company affiliated with Horizon Kinetics. It does not run an operating business. **[Fact]** It owns concentrated stakes in investment partnerships, exchange infrastructure (Miami International Holdings), Horizon Kinetics Holding Corp., and smaller fund and digital-asset positions. Revenue to shareholders comes from fair-value marks on those investments, advisory royalty interests, and fund economics, not from product sales. **[Fact]** As of February 28, 2026, total assets were $919.5 million and FRMO-attributable stockholders' equity was $376.7 million, about $8.55 per share. **[Fact]** Roughly 82% of FRMO's equity sits in a single consolidated investment position ("Investment A"). The stock trades OTC with thin liquidity.
why_market_wrong: The market prices FRMO like a volatile mark-to-market fund, not like a sum of parts. **[Inference]** OTC trading, fair-value earnings swings (Q3 FY2026 diluted EPS $1.89 vs a $0.54 loss a year earlier), and concentration in one line item push generalist investors away. **[Inference]** Private stakes (MIH post-IPO, HKHC, Winland, catalyst names) are likely carried below economic value in GAAP marks. Horizon Kinetics calls this a **dormant asset** case: value sits in subsidiaries and listing events, not in trailing GAAP earnings. There is no clean equity yield curve with one dated payoff; return depends on a catalyst stack over several years.
